FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: 5 stories spanning antitrust wins, regional M&A, CFPB confirmation drama, stablecoin consortium launch, and compliance settlements.

bullets.text: Klarna scores €2B antitrust win against Google in Swedish court over preferential treatment of Google's price comparison service versus Klarna-owned Prisjakt.

Senator Warren questions ex-Capital One executive Brian Johnson, CFPB nominee, on whether he influenced the bureau's decision to drop a lawsuit against his former employer—conflict-of-interest vote looming.
140+ members including BNY Mellon, Visa, and Coinbase launch low-cost stablecoin through Open Standard consortium to overhaul payments infrastructure.
EagleBank pays $9.7M to resolve BSA/AML violations; admits knowledge of customer check fraud and lax controls.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Vanguard outsources to T. Rowe Price, RIA consolidation continues, Hightower leadership shifts, and SEC eyes ETF regulation.

bullets.text: Vanguard taps T. Rowe Price as subadvisor on three active funds after internal quant strategies underperformed—a rare outsourcing move for the firm despite higher fees for investors.
Clearstead acquires $5.6B RIA team from myCIO Wealth Partners, continuing mid-market roll-up momentum as the firm expands its M&A appetite.
Hightower president Roberto Stewart departs amid Goldman Sachs-led sale process and investor pressure over delayed founder payouts, though firm adds key talent.
SEC signals regulatory tightening on ETFs with new rule proposals covering fund confidentiality and registration suspension as ETF assets hit $16T.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Reinsurance market awash with $790bn capital driving pricing pressure, while primary insurers face $24bn in Q2 losses and new MGAs target underserved segments.

bullets.text: Record $790bn reinsurance capital at mid-year renewals enabled double-digit pricing cuts on cat coverage—Kingstone just locked 15%+ savings on a $500m XoL limit with added wildfire protection.
Goldman Sachs pegs Q2'26 insured catastrophe losses at $24bn, with primary insurers absorbing 65-70% vs. reinsurers taking the rest.
Fidelis Partnership launches Adroit Underwriting MGA in Bermuda to capture underserved US SME casualty reinsurance through its Pine Walk platform.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Sovereign wealth funds doubling down on private credit while fintech/crypto narratives split between institutional adoption and litigation risk.

bullets.text: Sovereigns rotating hard into private credit—signaling sustained capital demand and LP tailwinds for specialty finance platforms.
Visa, Mastercard, US Bank, Google, Coinbase backing Open USD stablecoin—major payment infrastructure convergence on digital rails despite crypto headwinds.
NA private credit facing reputational/performance pressure while Europe remains resilient; regional market divergence widening on structure and regulation.
1,700 UK investors suing Binance for £150M+ damages—crypto litigation risk escalating as platforms face institutional legitimacy pressure.

PE & Deals.lede: PE fundraising hits new highs while distressed software becomes a target; secondaries market shifts toward leverage and evolving CV structures.

bullets.text: Top 100 North American PE firms raised nearly 10% more capital YoY, with KKR retaining the lead and Bain Capital jumping into the top five.
Arizona pension fund is temporarily increasing PE allocation to snap up distressed software assets facing valuation compression and debt maturity walls from peak-multiple deals.
Secondaries investors are now routinely embedding fund financing into continuation vehicles to juice returns, signaling a structural shift toward leverage in the secondary market.

Markets & Macro.lede: Regulatory upheaval dominates: EU crypto exodus accelerates, Supreme Court weakens financial regulators, while wealth transfer and private credit stress create new FIG opportunities and risks.

bullets.text: MiCA compliance deadline forces EU crypto exodus—majority of firms exiting or restructuring as only a small fraction secured required licences.
$84 trillion wealth transfer coming over two decades creates $60tn AUM opportunity, but asset managers face 70% historical client attrition at generational handoffs.
Medallia's equity wipeout and debt distress expose private credit's venture-into-growth playbook breaking down—broader model tensions signaled.
Supreme Court ruling expands presidential removal power over federal regulators, undermining agency independence across financial supervision and securities oversight.

Newsletters.lede: ETF market continues to surge past $23T, but SEC scrutinizing complex products while wealth transfer thesis faces healthcare headwinds.

bullets.text: Global ETF assets hit record $23T in May with inflows already exceeding last year's $739B pace YTD—momentum shows no signs of slowing.
SEC is actively soliciting public comment on novel ETF structures (leveraged, inverse, derivative-linked) amid tension between product innovation and investor protection.
Alternatives access expanding for retail/advisors as private-company lifecycles lengthen, but adoption rates remain murky despite growing distribution channels.
The $84T intergenerational wealth transfer thesis faces pressure: rising healthcare costs and extended lifespans could force retirees to spend down rather than pass along assets.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Four AI/tech stories span prediction-market vulnerabilities, LLM constraints, infrastructure costs, and hiring patterns—plus a social media negativity trend.

bullets.text: Risk Labs' decentralized prediction-market oracle is exploitable despite crypto-game-theory architecture; dozens of bettors have suffered losses from manipulation.
$1.5B Factory tackles the 'token budget problem'—LLMs hit context limits when processing large codebases, forcing accuracy/breadth trade-offs that slow enterprise AI adoption.
AI infrastructure costs fell 600x in 6 years, yet enterprise spending surges; the paradox: demand and architectural inefficiency, not pricing, drive rising bills.
Heavy AI-spending companies are hiring, not cutting headcount—Ramp data contradicts the automation-kills-jobs narrative dominating tech discourse.

all.lede: Five stories spanning alternatives democratization, Vanguard's rare outsourcing move, SEC scrutiny on complex ETFs, sovereign capital flooding private credit, and continued RIA consolidation.

bullets.text: Vanguard taps T. Rowe Price as subadvisor on three active funds after internal quants underperformed—a rare outsourcing move that signals even mega-managers are willing to pay up for external talent when strategies fail.
Sovereign wealth funds are aggressively rotating from public equities and bonds into private credit, signaling sustained institutional demand and capital availability for specialty finance platforms.
SEC opens comment period on novel ETF structures (leveraged, inverse, derivative-linked) as innovation outpaces regulatory guardrails—potential rule changes ahead on complex products.
Clearstead acquires $5.6B RIA team from myCIO in mid-market consolidation play, continuing the roll-up momentum as smaller wealth shops seek scale and acquirers hunt AUM.
